messages.content: Mission target: <TARGET_IP>. Begin the engagement now. Spawn the appropriate first agent.

{"thought": "SITUATION: Engagement starts with target <TARGET_IP>. No scan data available yet. Mission: find flag.txt. Phase indicates OSINT, but I need to understand the attack surface first. HYPOTHESIS: This appears to be a local network target (192.168.56.x). Without portscan data, I cannot identify exploitable services. OPTION A: Spawn scanner agent for comprehensive TCP/UDP port scan + service version detection — required before any exploitation. OPTION B: Attempt OSINT external search — unlikely to yield useful results for internal IP. OPTION C: Try to guess services and exploit directly — reckless, wastes time on wrong vectors. DECISION: Option A. A comprehensive scan is mandatory per my methodology: 'know everything before you exploit anything.' Scan will identify open ports, services, versions, and OS fingerprint. Based on results, I'll spawn exploit agents in parallel.", "action": "spawn_agent", "parameters": {"agent_type": "scanner", "target": "<TARGET_IP>", "task_type": "scan_tcp_udp_all_ports", "options": {"ports": "1-65535", "detect_versions": true, "detect_os": true, "udp_top_1000": true}}}

{"thought": "SITUATION: Scanner agent spawned for <TARGET_IP>. Scanning all TCP/UDP ports with version and OS detection. I need to wait for scan results before proceeding. No other agents can spawn until I know what services are running.", "action": "wait_for_agents", "parameters": {"agent_ids": ["scanner-ea28b19b"], "timeout": 1200}}

{"thought": "SITUATION: Scan complete. Target <TARGET_IP> is legacy-vulnerable-vm (deliberately vulnerable Linux VM). 27 open ports with multiple critical CVEs. Notable: vsftpd 2.3.4 (backdoor), Samba 3.0.20 (usermap_script), distccd (CVE-2004-2687), UnrealIRCd (backdoor), r-services (512-514). CRITICAL: Scanner notes port 1524 is a 'legacy-vulnerable-vm root shell' — direct bind shell providing root access. HYPOTHESES: (1) Port 1524 bindshell → immediate root shell (highest priority, scanner explicitly confirms). (2) vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor → CVE-2011-2523 → root on port 6200. (3) Samba usermap_script → CVE-2007-2447 → root shell. (4) distccd → CVE-2004-2687 → shell as distcc user. (5) UnrealIRCd backdoor → CVE-2010-2075 → IRCd user shell. OPTIONS: A) Connect to port 1524 directly — gets root immediately per scanner. B) Spawn only vsftpd — wastes known vectors. C) Spawn agents batch for all critical vectors in parallel — maximizes coverage. DECISION: Option C. I'll spawn exploit agents for all critical vectors simultaneously. Port 1524 is a bind shell on <TARGET_IP>:1524 — direct connection. While others run, I'll also connect to the bindshell. Flag likely at /home/msfadmin/flag.txt or /root/flag.txt.", "action": "spawn_agents_batch", "parameters": {"agents": [{"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "<TARGET_IP>", "task_type": "bindshell_1524", "options": {"port": 1524}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "<TARGET_IP>", "task_type": "exploit_vsftpd_21", "options": {"port": 21}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "<TARGET_IP>", "task_type": "exploit_samba_445", "options": {"port": 445}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "<TARGET_IP>", "task_type": "exploit_distcc_3632", "options": {"port": 3632}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "<TARGET_IP>", "task_type": "exploit_unrealircd_6667", "options": {"port": 6667}}]}}

{"thought": "SITUATION: 5 exploit agents spawned simultaneously targeting critical vulnerabilities on legacy-vulnerable-vm: (1) bindshell on port 1524 — direct root shell per scanner note, (2) vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor, (3) Samba 3.0.20 usermap_script, (4) distccd CVE-2004-2687, (5) UnrealIRCd backdoor. All are high-confidence RCE vectors. Waiting for any agent to succeed. First shell wins — I immediately proceed to post-exploitation to find flag.txt.", "action": "wait_for_agents", "parameters": {"agent_ids": "all", "timeout": 300}}
